deck: Jonathan Jackson and Amie Vaccaro introduce Impact Delivery, Dimagi's approach to technology for global health and development that optimizes long term impact while accepting the real constraints of the work: better impact, more impact, and sustained impact.
seo_description: Jonathan Jackson and Amie Vaccaro introduce Impact Delivery: Dimagi's approach to better, more, and sustained impact from digital in global health.
og_description: Introducing Impact Delivery: better impact, more impact, and sustained impact from digital in global health and development.

pull_quote.text: We already can be doing more today with the money we're spending. And we should all be expecting more. More value for money, more impact, better jobs.
pull_quote.cite: Jonathan Jackson on raising the bar for digital

bullets: Why digital falls short: huge potential, yet many projects add burden, drive little obvious impact, and never prove value for money
The three pillars of Impact Delivery: better impact, more impact, and sustained impact
Better impact: designing technology that makes the provider's job easier and the client's experience better, with data flowing back as exhaust from the work itself
More impact: building on a configurable platform so programs can add new use cases, integrations, and user types over time without ballooning costs
Sustained impact: articulating value for money with all stakeholders and planning for the system to still matter in year three, year five, and beyond
Partner, not vendor: why complex service delivery needs long term partnership over a check-the-box deployment
Process and people: the product owner mindset, security-first foundations, and the mindset of simply expecting more from digital

stats.label: of Dimagi experience the Impact Delivery approach is built on
ROIs that non-specialist providers like community health workers can deliver
